questions.text: The direction of magnetic field lines outside a bar magnet is from:
questions.explanation: By convention, magnetic field lines emerge from the North pole and merge at the South pole outside the magnet.

questions.text: Inside a bar magnet, the direction of magnetic field lines is from:
questions.explanation: Magnetic field lines are closed curves; they travel from South to North inside the magnet.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a property of magnetic field lines?
options: They are crowded near the poles
They represent the direction of electric current
questions.explanation: Magnetic field lines represent the magnetic field, not the electric current itself.

questions.text: Why do two magnetic field lines never intersect each other?
options: At the point of intersection, a compass would show two directions
They are too weak to cross
questions.explanation: If they intersected, the compass needle would point in two different directions at the same point, which is impossible.

questions.text: Who discovered that a current-carrying wire produces a magnetic field around it?
questions.explanation: In 1820, Oersted noticed a compass needle deflected when placed near a wire carrying electric current.

questions.text: The rule used to find the direction of the magnetic field around a straight current-carrying conductor is:
questions.explanation: According to the Right-Hand Thumb Rule, if the thumb points in the direction of current, the fingers wrap in the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.text: The magnetic field lines around a straight current-carrying conductor are in the shape of:
questions.explanation: The field lines form circles centered on the wire, lying in a plane perpendicular to the wire.

questions.text: If the current through a straight conductor is increased, the strength of the magnetic field:
questions.explanation: The magnitude of the magnetic field produced at a given point is directly proportional to the current passing through the wire.

questions.text: The strength of the magnetic field produced by a straight wire ________ as the distance from the wire increases.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance from the conductor.

questions.text: At the center of a current-carrying circular loop, the magnetic field lines appear as:
questions.explanation: Near the center of the loop, the arcs of the big circles appear as straight lines.

questions.text: A coil of many circular turns of insulated copper wire wrapped closely in the shape of a cylinder is called a:
questions.explanation: A solenoid produces a magnetic field similar to that of a bar magnet when current flows through it.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid carrying current is:
questions.explanation: The field lines inside the solenoid are parallel straight lines, indicating a uniform magnetic field.

questions.text: A strong magnetic field produced inside a solenoid can be used to magnetize a piece of magnetic material like soft iron. The magnet so formed is called:
questions.explanation: An electromagnet is a temporary magnet that works only as long as electric current flows.

questions.text: Which of the following materials is most suitable for the core of an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: Soft iron loses its magnetism quickly when the current is switched off, making it ideal for electromagnets.

questions.text: The force experienced by a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field is maximum when the angle between them is:
questions.explanation: The force is maximum when the direction of current is perpendicular to the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.text: According to Fleming's Left-Hand Rule, the forefinger points in the direction of:
questions.explanation: The forefinger represents the field, the middle finger represents current, and the thumb represents motion/force.

questions.text: In Fleming's Left-Hand Rule, the thumb points in the direction of:
questions.explanation: The thumb indicates the direction of the force acting on the conductor (or its motion).

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic field strength (magnetic induction) is:
questions.explanation: Tesla (T) is the SI unit named after Nikola Tesla.

questions.text: A positively-charged particle (alpha-particle) projected towards west is deflected towards north by a magnetic field. The direction of magnetic field is:
questions.explanation: Using Fleming's Left-Hand Rule (Current = West, Force = North), the thumb and fingers show the field is upward.

questions.text: In India, the potential difference (Voltage) of domestic AC supply is:
questions.explanation: The standard domestic power supply in India is 220 Volts.

questions.text: The frequency of Alternating Current (AC) supplied in India is:
questions.explanation: The AC in India changes direction 100 times per second, which means its frequency is 50 Hz.

questions.text: What is the color of the insulation for the live wire in a domestic circuit?
questions.explanation: By standard convention, the live wire is insulated in red (or brown).

questions.text: The neutral wire in a domestic circuit usually has insulation of color:
questions.explanation: The neutral wire is typically black (or blue).

questions.text: The earth wire is used as a safety measure to prevent electric shocks. Its insulation color is:
questions.explanation: The earth wire is green (or yellow-green).

questions.text: When the live wire and neutral wire come into direct contact, it results in a:
questions.explanation: Direct contact between live and neutral wires leads to a sudden high current flow called a short circuit.

questions.text: Connecting too many electrical appliances to a single socket can lead to:
questions.explanation: Overloading occurs when the total current drawn exceeds the capacity of the wires.

questions.text: An electric fuse is a safety device based on the ________ effect of electric current.
questions.explanation: When excessive current flows, the fuse wire heats up and melts, breaking the circuit.

questions.text: The main advantage of Alternating Current (AC) over Direct Current (DC) is:
options: AC can be transmitted over long distances with low energy loss
DC cannot be used for heating
questions.explanation: AC voltage can be easily stepped up or down, allowing efficient long-distance transmission.

questions.text: What does a compass needle represent?
questions.explanation: A compass needle is essentially a small bar magnet whose ends point toward the North and South.

questions.text: The magnetic field is a quantity that has:
questions.explanation: The magnetic field is a vector quantity, possessing both size (strength) and direction.

questions.text: Magnetic field lines are ________ near the poles where the magnetic field is strongest.
questions.explanation: The density of field lines indicates the relative strength of the magnetic field.

questions.text: If a current-carrying straight conductor is held vertically and current flows upwards, the magnetic field is ________ (viewed from above).
questions.explanation: By the Right-Hand Thumb Rule, the field lines wrap anti-clockwise when the current is upward.

questions.text: The magnetic field lines produced by a circular loop are ________ at the center of the loop.
questions.explanation: At the center of a circular loop, the field lines are perpendicular to the plane of the loop and appear straight.

questions.text: Which property of a proton can change while it moves freely in a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Velocity (and momentum) changes because the magnetic force changes the direction of motion, even if speed remains constant. (Note: Multi-choice might accept both C and D).

questions.text: The pattern of magnetic field lines around a current-carrying solenoid is similar to that of a:
questions.explanation: One end of the solenoid acts as a North pole and the other as a South pole, just like a bar magnet.

questions.text: The purpose of the 'earth wire' in a domestic circuit is to provide a low-resistance path for:
questions.explanation: Earthing ensures that any leakage current from the metallic body of an appliance flows to the earth, protecting the user.

questions.text: The frequency of Direct Current (DC) is:
questions.explanation: DC flows in only one direction and does not change polarity, so its frequency is zero.

questions.text: Magnetic field strength is measured in the vicinity of a solenoid. It is strongest:
options: At the ends of the solenoid
questions.explanation: The magnetic field is highly concentrated and uniform inside the solenoid.

questions.text: In Fleming's Left-Hand Rule, the middle finger points in the direction of:
questions.explanation: The middle finger represents the direction of the electric current.

questions.text: A potential difference between the live wire and the neutral wire in Indian domestic circuits is:
questions.explanation: The standard voltage difference maintained between live and neutral wires is 220V.
